chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Crl. Bail Application No. 675 of 2023 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGES For hearing of bail application. 27-04-2023 M/s. Mehboob-ur-Rehman and Muhammad Farhan, Advocates for applicant. Mr. Faheem Hussain Panhwar, DPG. ============= Omar Sial, J: Affan-ur-Rahim has sought post arrest bail in crime number 123 of 2023 registered under sections 381, 420, 406, 506-B and 34 P.P.C. at the Awami Colony police station. Earlier, his application seeking bail was dismissed on 13.03.2023 by the learned 14th Additional Sessions Judge, Karachi East. 2. A background to the case is that the aforementioned F.I.R. was registered on 09.02.2023 on the complaint of Mohammad Imran. Imran reported an incident which had occurred on 23.01.2023. He recorded that the applicant worked as a sales manager in his business. Imran recorded that he received complaints that the applicant was spoiling the business reputation and that he had also embezzled money and stolen computer equipment. The applicant also hacked the computer system of the complainant and used it for his own benefit. On 26.01.2023, 2 unknown persons had come to the complainant’s office and threatened him. 3. I have heard the learned counsel for the applicant and the learned DPG. 4. The learned DPG has expressed his inability to show any evidence that supports the allegations made against the applicant in the F.I.R. Such is the apathy that no accounts demonstrating embezzled funds or statements recorded of witnesses cheated have been collected in the investigation. 2 There is no evidence to show that hardware or software was stolen or systems hacked by the applicant.

The learned DPG has expressed his inability to show any evidence that supports the allegations made against the applicant in the F.I.R. Such is the apathy that no accounts demonstrating embezzled funds or statements recorded of witnesses cheated have been collected in the investigation. 2 There is no evidence to show that hardware or software was stolen or systems hacked by the applicant. No trail of any money allegedly stolen by the applicant is on record. The case of the applicant is one of further inquiry. 5. Above are the reasons for the short order dated 13.04.2023. JUDGE
